Operation 'GetContactByIdentifiers' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The original summary 'Get Contacts by Identifiers' is too generic and doesn't convey the key distinction of exact matching. The description is verbose and repetitive with status code explanations scattered throughout. The improved summary emphasizes 'exact identifier values' to help AI agents understand when to use this endpoint versus the general Search endpoint. The revised description consolidates information logically: purpose, supported identifier types, use case differentiation, response handling (including partial success scenarios), and error conditions. This structure improves AI agent interpretability by clearly indicating what inputs are expected, what outputs to expect, and how to handle different response scenarios.

data.current_description:
Use this endpoint to retrieve up to 100 contacts that match the requested identifier values for a single identifier type.

`identifier_type` must be a valid identifier type: `email`, `phone_number_id`, `external_id`, or `anonymous_id`.

Use this endpoint instead of the [Search Contacts endpoint](https://www.twilio.com/docs/sendgrid/api-reference/contacts/search-contacts) when you can provide exact identifiers and do not need to include other [Segmentation Query Language (SGQL)](https://www.twilio.com/docs/sendgrid/for-developers/sending-email/segmentation-query-language/) filters when searching.

This endpoint returns a `200` status code when any contacts match the identifiers you supplied. When searching multiple identifier values in a single request, it is possible that some will match a contact while others will not. When a partially successful search like this is made, the matching contacts are returned in an object and an error message is returned for the identifier values that are not found.

This endpoint returns a `404` status code when no contacts are found for the provided identifiers.

This endpoint returns a `400` status code if any searched addresses are invalid.

Twilio SendGrid recommends exporting your contacts regularly as a backup to avoid issues or lost data.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve contacts by exact identifier values
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves up to 100 contacts matching the provided identifier values for a single identifier type. Supports identifier types: `email`, `phone_number_id`, `external_id`, or `anonymous_id`. This endpoint is optimized for exact identifier matching without additional SGQL filters. Returns matching contacts with a `200` status code; partial matches return both matching contacts and error messages for unmatched identifiers. Returns `404` when no contacts match the provided identifiers, and `400` if any identifiers are invalid. Note: Some identifier values in a batch request may match while others do not, resulting in partial success responses.
data.reasoning: The original summary 'Get Contacts by Identifiers' is too generic and doesn't convey the key distinction of exact matching. The description is verbose and repetitive with status code explanations scattered throughout. The improved summary emphasizes 'exact identifier values' to help AI agents understand when to use this endpoint versus the general Search endpoint. The revised description consolidates information logically: purpose, supported identifier types, use case differentiation, response handling (including partial success scenarios), and error conditions. This structure improves AI agent interpretability by clearly indicating what inputs are expected, what outputs to expect, and how to handle different response scenarios.

message: Operation 'UpdateContact' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Summary is too generic ('Add or Update a Contact'). The improved summary emphasizes the upsert operation, the scale (30,000 contacts), and the asynchronous nature, which are critical for AI agents to understand when to use this operation. The description is comprehensive and already contains necessary details about job_id tracking, identifier requirements, and email normalization.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows the [upsert](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/upsert) (insert or update) of up to 30,000 contacts, or 6MB of data, whichever is lower**.
Because the creation and update of contacts is an asynchronous process, the response will not contain immediate feedback on the processing of your upserted contacts. Rather, it will contain an HTTP 202 response indicating the contacts are queued for processing or an HTTP 4XX error containing validation errors. Should you wish to get the resulting contact's ID or confirm that your contacts have been updated or added, you can use the [Get Contacts by Identifiers operation](https://www.twilio.com/docs/sendgrid/api-reference/contacts/get-contacts-by-identifiers).
Please note that custom fields need to have been already created if you wish to set their values for the contacts being upserted. To do this, please use the [Create Custom Field Definition endpoint](https://www.twilio.com/docs/sendgrid/api-reference/custom-fields/create-custom-field-definition).
You will see a `job_id` in the response to your request. This can be used to check the status of your upsert job. To do so, please use the [Import Contacts Status endpoint](https://www.twilio.com/docs/sendgrid/api-reference/contacts/import-contacts-status).
If the contact already exists in the system, any entries submitted via this endpoint will update the existing contact. In order to update a contact, all of its existing identifiers must be present. Any fields omitted from the request will remain as they were. A contact's ID cannot be used to update the contact.
The email field will be changed to all lower-case. If a contact is added with an email that exists but contains capital letters, the existing contact with the all lower-case email will be updated.
data.reasoning: Summary is too generic ('Add or Update a Contact'). The improved summary emphasizes the upsert operation, the scale (30,000 contacts), and the asynchronous nature, which are critical for AI agents to understand when to use this operation. The description is comprehensive and already contains necessary details about job_id tracking, identifier requirements, and email normalization.

message: Operation 'DeleteContact' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Summary lacks specificity about the asynchronous nature and parameter options. Description is missing explicit output details (HTTP 202 response, job_id return value) which are critical for AI agents to understand the operation's behavior and how to track completion.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint can be used to delete one or more contacts**.

The query parameter `ids` must set to a comma-separated list of contact IDs for bulk contact deletion.

The query parameter `delete_all_contacts` must be set to `"true"` to delete **all** contacts. 

You must set either `ids` or `delete_all_contacts`.

Deletion jobs are processed asynchronously.

Twilio SendGrid recommends exporting your contacts regularly as a backup to avoid issues or lost data.
data.summary_suggestion: Delete one or more contacts asynchronously
data.description_suggestion: Deletes one or more contacts by ID or deletes all contacts. Requires either the `ids` query parameter (comma-separated contact IDs for bulk deletion) or `delete_all_contacts` set to "true" to delete all contacts. Deletion is processed asynchronously and returns HTTP 202 with a job_id for status tracking. At least one parameter must be provided. Returns a job_id that can be used to monitor deletion status.
data.reasoning: Summary lacks specificity about the asynchronous nature and parameter options. Description is missing explicit output details (HTTP 202 response, job_id return value) which are critical for AI agents to understand the operation's behavior and how to track completion.

message: Operation 'ListContact' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, unclear_purpose, missing_context. Description lacks context about when to use this endpoint versus alternatives (e.g., batch retrieval). Improved summary emphasizes the limit and recency, while description clarifies the use case and relationship to other endpoints for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint will return up to 50 of the most recent contacts uploaded or attached to a list**. 

This list will then be sorted by email address.

The full contact count is also returned.

Please note that pagination of the contacts has been deprecated.

Twilio SendGrid recommends exporting your contacts regularly as a backup to avoid issues or lost data.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve up to 50 most recent contacts
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves up to 50 of the most recent contacts in your account, sorted by email address. Returns contact details along with the total contact count. Note that pagination has been deprecated; use the Batch Contacts endpoint for retrieving specific contacts by ID. This endpoint is useful for sampling or auditing recent contact activity.
data.reasoning: Summary 'Get Sample Contacts' is vague and doesn't clarify the operation's actual purpose or constraints. Description lacks context about when to use this endpoint versus alternatives (e.g., batch retrieval). Improved summary emphasizes the limit and recency, while description clarifies the use case and relationship to other endpoints for better AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'ListBatchedContact' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Summary is clear and appropriate. Description lacks explicit details about input format flexibility ('array or one or more strings' is unclear) and missing output details (what is returned, how missing contacts are handled). data.current_description:
**This endpoint is used to retrieve a set of contacts identified by their IDs.**

This can be more efficient endpoint to get contacts than making a series of individual `GET` requests to the "Get a Contact by ID" endpoint.

You can supply up to 100 IDs. Pass them into the `ids` field in your request body as an array or one or more strings.

Twilio SendGrid recommends exporting your contacts regularly as a backup to avoid issues or lost data.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves up to 100 contacts by their IDs in a single request. More efficient than making multiple individual GET requests. Accepts an array of contact IDs in the request body `ids` field. Returns an array of contact objects with full details for each requested contact. Contacts not found are excluded from the response.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and appropriate. Description lacks explicit details about input format flexibility ('array or one or more strings' is unclear) and missing output details (what is returned, how missing contacts are handled). Improved description clarifies input requirements and specifies the response structure for better AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'DeleteContactIdentifier' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Summary is clear and appropriate. Description is missing explicit input details (path parameter, request body structure) and output details (HTTP 202 response, job_id). Improved description specifies all required inputs and expected response format for AI agents to properly construct and interpret the operation.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint can be used to delete one identifier from a contact.**

Deletion jobs are processed asynchronously.

Note this is different from deleting a contact. If the contact has only one identifier, the asynchronous request will fail. All contacts are required to have at least one identifier.

The request body field `identifier_type` must have a valid value of "EMAIL", "PHONENUMBERID", "EXTERNALID", or "ANONYMOUSID".
data.description_suggestion: Deletes a single identifier from a contact asynchronously. Requires the contact_id path parameter and a request body specifying the `identifier_type` (must be one of: "EMAIL", "PHONENUMBERID", "EXTERNALID", or "ANONYMOUSID"). The operation will fail if the contact has only one identifier, as all contacts must retain at least one identifier. Returns HTTP 202 with a job_id for tracking deletion status. This operation differs from deleting the entire contact.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and appropriate. Description is missing explicit input details (path parameter, request body structure) and output details (HTTP 202 response, job_id). Improved description specifies all required inputs and expected response format for AI agents to properly construct and interpret the operation.

message: Operation 'ExportContact' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Summary 'Export Contacts' is too generic and doesn't indicate the asynchronous nature or that it initiates a job. Description is comprehensive but lacks clarity about the immediate response (job ID, upload_uri). Added summary specificity and clarified that this is an asynchronous operation returning a job ID for status tracking.

data.current_description:
**Use this endpoint to export lists or segments of contacts**.

If you would just like to have a link to the exported list sent to your email set the `notifications.email` option to `true` in the `POST` payload.

If you would like to download the list, take the `id` that is returned and use the "Export Contacts Status" endpoint to get the `urls`. Once you have the list of URLs, make a `GET` request to each URL provided to download your CSV file(s).

You specify the segments and or/contact lists you wish to export by providing the relevant IDs in, respectively, the `segment_ids` and `list_ids` fields in the request body.

The lists will be provided in either JSON or CSV files. To specify which of these you would required, set the request body `file_type` field to `json` or `csv`.

You can also specify a maximum file size (in MB). If the export file is larger than this, it will be split into multiple files.
data.summary_suggestion: Initiate contact export job for lists or segments
data.description_suggestion: Initiates an asynchronous export job for contacts from specified lists and/or segments. Accepts `segment_ids` and `list_ids` in the request body to identify which contacts to export. Supports JSON or CSV file formats via the `file_type` parameter. Optionally specify a maximum file size (in MB) to split large exports into multiple files. Returns a job ID and optional email notification URL. Set `notifications.email` to `true` to receive a download link via email. Use the returned job ID with the "Export Contacts Status" endpoint to retrieve download URLs once processing completes.
data.reasoning: Summary 'Export Contacts' is too generic and doesn't indicate the asynchronous nature or that it initiates a job. Description is comprehensive but lacks clarity about the immediate response (job ID, upload_uri). Added summary specificity and clarified that this is an asynchronous operation returning a job ID for status tracking.

message: Operation 'GetExportContact' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details. Summary 'Export Contacts Status' is vague and doesn't indicate the dual purpose of retrieving both status and download URLs. Description lacks clarity about the path parameter requirement and status field values. Enhanced summary to be more action-oriented and improved description to specify the path parameter requirement and clarify the status values for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint can be used to check the status of a contact export job**. 

To use this call, you will need the `id` from the "Export Contacts" call.

If you would like to download a list, take the `id` that is returned from the "Export Contacts" endpoint and make an API request here to get the `urls`. Once you have the list of URLs, make a `GET` request on each URL to download your CSV file(s).

Twilio SendGrid recommends exporting your contacts regularly as a backup to avoid issues or lost data.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve export job status and download URLs
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the current status and details of a contact export job using the job ID from the "Export Contacts" endpoint. Returns the job's `status` field indicating processing stage (pending, completed, errored, or failed), `export_type`, and when ready, a `urls` field containing download links for the exported file(s). Use the returned URLs to download the exported contact data. Requires the export job ID as a path parameter.
data.reasoning: Summary 'Export Contacts Status' is vague and doesn't indicate the dual purpose of retrieving both status and download URLs. Description lacks clarity about the path parameter requirement and status field values. Enhanced summary to be more action-oriented and improved description to specify the path parameter requirement and clarify the status values for better AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'ImportContact' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is adequate and action-oriented. Description is comprehensive but lacks clarity about the immediate response structure (job_id, upload_uri, upload_headers) and doesn't explicitly state the side effects of contact creation/updating. Enhanced description to clarify the two-step process response and explicitly document the side effects of contact creation and updating for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows a CSV upload containing up to one million contacts or 5GB of data, whichever is smaller. At least one identifier is required for a successful import.**

Imports take place asynchronously: the endpoint returns a URL (`upload_uri`) and HTTP headers (`upload_headers`) which can subsequently be used to `PUT` a file of contacts to be imported into our system.

Uploaded CSV files may also be [gzip-compressed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gzip).

In either case, you must include the field `file_type` with the value `csv` in your request body.

The `field_mappings` parameter is a respective list of field definition IDs to map the uploaded CSV columns to. It allows you to use CSVs where one or more columns are skipped (`null`) or remapped to the contact field.

For example, if `field_mappings` is set to `[null, "w1", "_rf1"]`, this means skip column 0, map column 1 to the custom field with the ID `w1`, and map column 2 to the reserved field with the ID `_rf1`. See the "Get All Field Definitions" endpoint to fetch your custom and reserved field IDs to use with `field_mappings`.

Once you receive the response body you can then initiate a **second** API call where you use the supplied URL and HTTP header to upload your file. For example:

`curl --upload-file "file/path.csv" "URL_GIVEN" -H "HEADER_GIVEN"`

If you would like to monitor the status of your import job, use the `job_id` and the "Import Contacts Status" endpoint.

Twilio SendGrid recommends exporting your contacts regularly as a backup to avoid issues or lost data.
data.description_suggestion: Initiates an asynchronous contact import job and returns upload credentials. Accepts CSV files up to 5GB or one million contacts (whichever is smaller). Requires at least one contact identifier field. Returns a `job_id`, `upload_uri`, and `upload_headers` for a subsequent PUT request to upload the CSV file. Supports gzip-compressed files. Use `field_mappings` to map CSV columns to contact fields, skip columns with `null`, or remap to custom/reserved fields. Monitor import progress using the returned `job_id` with the "Import Contacts Status" endpoint. Side effects: Creates new contacts or updates existing contacts based on identifier matching.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate and action-oriented. Description is comprehensive but lacks clarity about the immediate response structure (job_id, upload_uri, upload_headers) and doesn't explicitly state the side effects of contact creation/updating. Enhanced description to clarify the two-step process response and explicitly document the side effects of contact creation and updating for better AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'SearchContact' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Summary 'Search Contacts' is too generic and doesn't indicate the use of SGQL. Description lacks clarity about the response limit (50 contacts) and doesn't mention the alternative endpoint for email-only searches. Enhanced summary to specify SGQL usage and improved description to clarify the 50-contact limit, timeout behavior, and recommend the email-specific endpoint for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description:
**Use this endpoint to locate contacts**.

The request body's `query` field accepts valid [SGQL](https://sendgrid.com/docs/for-developers/sending-email/segmentation-query-language/) for searching for a contact.

Because contact emails are stored in lower case, using SGQL to search by email address requires the provided email address to be in lower case. The SGQL `lower()` function can be used for this.

Only the first 50 contacts that meet the search criteria will be returned.

If the query takes longer than 20 seconds, a `408 Request Timeout` status will be returned.

Formatting the `created_at` and `updated_at` values as Unix timestamps is deprecated. Instead, they are returned as ISO format as string.
data.summary_suggestion: Search contacts using SGQL query language
data.description_suggestion: Searches for contacts using Segmentation Query Language (SGQL) in the request body's `query` field. Returns up to 50 matching contacts. Email addresses are stored in lowercase; use the SGQL `lower()` function when searching by email. Queries exceeding 20 seconds timeout with a 408 status. Note: `created_at` and `updated_at` are returned as ISO format strings, not Unix timestamps. For exact email searches without SGQL filters, use the "Get Contacts by Emails" endpoint instead.
data.reasoning: Summary 'Search Contacts' is too generic and doesn't indicate the use of SGQL. Description lacks clarity about the response limit (50 contacts) and doesn't mention the alternative endpoint for email-only searches. Enhanced summary to specify SGQL usage and improved description to clarify the 50-contact limit, timeout behavior, and recommend the email-specific endpoint for better AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'ListContactByEmail' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Summary 'Get Contacts by Emails' is vague about the quantity limit and doesn't indicate it's an exact match search. Description is comprehensive but overly verbose with redundant information about backup recommendations. Enhanced summary to specify the 100-contact limit and improved description to be more concise while clarifying response codes, partial match behavior, and when to use this endpoint versus the search endpoint.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to retrieve up to 100 contacts matching the searched `email` address(es), including any `alternate_emails`.** 

Email addresses are unique to a contact, meaning this endpoint can treat an email address as a primary key to search by. The contact object associated with the address, whether it is their `email` or one of their `alternate_emails` will be returned if matched.

Email addresses in the search request do not need to match the case in which they're stored, but the email addresses in the result will be all lower case. Empty strings are excluded from the search and will not be returned.

This endpoint should be used in place of the "Search Contacts" endpoint when you can provide exact email addresses and do not need to include other [Segmentation Query Language (SGQL)](https://sendgrid.com/docs/for-developers/sending-email/segmentation-query-language/) filters when searching.

If you need to access a large percentage of your contacts, we recommend exporting your contacts with the "Export Contacts" endpoint and filtering the client side results.

This endpoint returns a `200` status code when any contacts match the address(es) you supplied. When searching multiple addresses in a single request, it is possible that some addresses will match a contact while others will not. When a partially successful search like this is made, the matching contacts are returned in an object and an error message is returned for the email address(es) that are not found. 

This endpoint returns a `404` status code when no contacts are found for the provided email address(es).

A `400` status code is returned if any searched addresses are invalid.

Twilio SendGrid recommends exporting your contacts regularly as a backup to avoid issues or lost data.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve up to 100 contacts by email addresses
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves up to 100 contacts matching the provided email address(es), including matches against `alternate_emails`. Email addresses are case-insensitive in requests but returned in lowercase. Searches treat email as a unique identifier. Returns `200` with matching contacts when any addresses match; returns `404` when no matches found; returns `400` for invalid email addresses. Partial matches return both matching contacts and error messages for unmatched addresses. Empty strings are excluded from searches. Prefer this endpoint over "Search Contacts" for exact email lookups without SGQL filters.
data.reasoning: Summary 'Get Contacts by Emails' is vague about the quantity limit and doesn't indicate it's an exact match search. Description is comprehensive but overly verbose with redundant information about backup recommendations. Enhanced summary to specify the 100-contact limit and improved description to be more concise while clarifying response codes, partial match behavior, and when to use this endpoint versus the search endpoint.
